content: ICM curiosity mechanisms can be repurposed to actively prune low-relevance information from an LLM agent's working memory, directly mitigating the reasoning breakdown caused by working memory capacity limits by prioritizing only intrinsically novel, task-relevant content for retention. This creates a self-regulating working memory system that automatically fits within the agent's inherent capacity bounds without manual context window truncation.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
